name: Rutgers University - New Brunswick
strategic_role: NJ public flagship/value premed baseline
why_included: Defines the NJ in-state public flagship baseline against which private, elite, and out-of-state options should be compared.
scorecard_notes: Scorecard earnings are broad school-level outcomes and should not be interpreted as premed-specific outcomes. Median debt should be verified directly against the Scorecard API or data download.
cost_notes: Strong in-state public baseline. Any higher-cost private or out-of-state option should justify its incremental cost over Rutgers.
prehealth_advising_notes: Rutgers has a formal Health Professions Office serving students and alumni pursuing medicine, dentistry, PA, optometry, podiatry, and other health professions.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Rutgers Health ecosystem includes Rutgers Robert Wood Johnson Medical School in New Brunswick and Rutgers New Jersey Medical School in Newark.
research_access_notes: Large public research university with substantial research opportunities, but students may need to self-advocate.
early_assurance_notes: Rutgers-New Brunswick has BA/MD pathways for current Rutgers students rather than a high-school senior BA/MD path. Do not confuse this with Rutgers-Newark BA/MD.
gpa_risk_notes: Large school with competitive intro science courses; strong students can thrive, but advising and opportunity access may require initiative.
student_fit_notes: Large flagship environment. Best for a student comfortable navigating a big public university.
parent_roi_note: This is the key NJ public baseline. It may be hard for expensive schools to beat unless they offer a clearly better fit, aid package, direct-med path, or distinctive ecosystem.

name: The College of New Jersey
strategic_role: Smaller NJ public value pre-health option with direct-med relevance
why_included: Represents a smaller public undergraduate-focused alternative to Rutgers, with a highly relevant TCNJ / Rutgers NJMS seven-year program.
scorecard_notes: Scorecard and secondary Scorecard-derived values should be verified directly before final release.
cost_notes: Strong NJ public value option. Slightly different value proposition than Rutgers because of smaller size and undergraduate focus.
prehealth_advising_notes: TCNJ has a Medical Careers Advisory Committee supporting students pursuing medical, dental, and other health-profession paths.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Not as hospital-dense as urban medical ecosystem schools, but strategically important because of the Rutgers NJMS seven-year program.
research_access_notes: Likely more undergraduate-focused than a large research university; verify specific research access and pre-health outcomes.
early_assurance_notes: TCNJ / Rutgers NJMS seven-year program is a major reason this school belongs in the app.
gpa_risk_notes: Premed coursework is still demanding, but smaller-school fit may help some students.
student_fit_notes: Potentially strong for a student who wants a smaller public college environment rather than a large flagship.
parent_roi_note: TCNJ may be compelling if Lucy would thrive more in a smaller public environment than at Rutgers.

name: Rowan University
strategic_role: Practical NJ public health-professions ecosystem option
why_included: Represents a practical NJ public healthcare ecosystem with MD and DO accelerated pathway relevance.
scorecard_notes: School-level earnings and graduation metrics are lower than some other NJ anchors, but Rowan has distinct healthcare-pathway relevance.
cost_notes: Public NJ option with practical healthcare pathway value. Not the strongest broad ROI signal, but strategically relevant.
prehealth_advising_notes: Rowan has an Office of Pre-Health supporting medicine, dentistry, OT, optometry, PT, PA, podiatry, veterinary medicine, and other health paths.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Relevant because of Cooper Medical School of Rowan University and Rowan-Virtua School of Osteopathic Medicine ecosystem.
research_access_notes: Verify undergraduate research access, especially through health sciences and medical-school-related opportunities.
early_assurance_notes: Rowan has 3+4 accelerated pathways involving CMSRU MD and Rowan-Virtua SOM DO options. Future data should split MD and DO pathways.
gpa_risk_notes: Premed path still requires strong grades. Accelerated programs may have structured continuation requirements.
student_fit_notes: Good fit if Lucy likes Rowan and is open to a practical NJ health-professions environment.
parent_roi_note: Rowan should be framed as a practical NJ health ecosystem option, not simply as a broad prestige or earnings play.

name: New Jersey Institute of Technology
strategic_role: NJ public STEM, health-tech, and BS/MD-relevant option
why_included: Represents the public STEM/health-tech lane and has a Rutgers NJMS accelerated BS/MD pathway.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings likely reflect STEM, engineering, computing, and technology outcomes. Do not interpret as premed-specific.
cost_notes: Official tuition and fee data were researched, but total COA should be verified before final app release.
prehealth_advising_notes: NJIT has pre-health advising and a committee-letter process. Best interpreted through a STEM/health-tech lens rather than classic biology-premed only.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Urban Newark location and NJMS linkage matter, but clinical ecosystem access should be verified.
research_access_notes: Strong STEM/research and health-tech potential, especially if Lucy is interested in biomedical engineering, data, computing, or AI-related healthcare.
early_assurance_notes: NJIT / Rutgers NJMS accelerated BS/MD is a major reason for inclusion.
gpa_risk_notes: STEM-heavy environment can create GPA pressure for a premed student.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy actually likes STEM, engineering, computing, biomedical science, or health-tech culture.
parent_roi_note: NJIT has a strong ROI signal and good fallback value, but it is not automatically the best classic premed environment.

name: Rutgers University - Newark
strategic_role: Urban NJ public / NJMS-proximity direct-med option
why_included: Represents an urban Rutgers option with Rutgers NJMS proximity and a high-school BA/MD pathway.
scorecard_notes: Graduation rate is meaningfully lower than Rutgers New Brunswick and TCNJ in the researched data. Median earnings and debt need direct Scorecard verification.
cost_notes: Lower direct cost than Rutgers New Brunswick in official admissions data, but fit and graduation-rate context matter.
prehealth_advising_notes: Rutgers-Newark has pre-health advising and a BA/MD program with Rutgers NJMS.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Urban Newark location and Rutgers NJMS proximity are the main strategic differentiators.
research_access_notes: Verify undergraduate research and clinical access directly.
early_assurance_notes: Rutgers-Newark BA/MD is a high-school senior pathway. This should be distinguished from Rutgers-New Brunswick pathways for current Rutgers students.
gpa_risk_notes: Urban public setting and premed coursework require fit and self-direction.
student_fit_notes: Campus/location fit is central. This is not interchangeable with Rutgers New Brunswick.
parent_roi_note: Rutgers-Newark is useful because of NJMS proximity and BA/MD relevance, but it should not be treated as simply Rutgers-New Brunswick at a lower price.

name: Stevens Institute of Technology
strategic_role: Private STEM/high-ROI/health-tech option with Rutgers NJMS accelerated premedicine relevance
why_included: Represents the private STEM/high-earnings lane and provides a NJ-area comparison against NJIT, Northeastern, Drexel, Case Western, and MIT-style health-tech paths.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings likely reflect engineering, computing, finance, tech, and STEM outcomes. This is useful fallback value but not premed-specific.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost. Actual value depends on net price and whether Lucy wants a STEM-heavy environment.
prehealth_advising_notes: Stevens has pre-health advising and an accelerated pre-medicine path with Rutgers NJMS.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Hoboken/NYC-area context may be helpful, but Stevens is best understood as STEM/health-tech rather than hospital-density premed.
research_access_notes: Strong STEM and technology orientation. Useful for biomedical engineering, health tech, data, AI, devices, or non-med STEM fallback.
early_assurance_notes: Accelerated pre-medicine program with Rutgers NJMS is a high-reach direct-med-style path. Verify current requirements and class-rank discrepancy.
gpa_risk_notes: STEM rigor may create GPA pressure for premed.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy would like Stevens even if she pivoted away from medicine.
parent_roi_note: Strong earnings and STEM fallback value, but expensive and not automatically better for classic premed than lower-cost NJ options.

name: Seton Hall University
strategic_role: Private NJ pre-health option with Hackensack Meridian guaranteed-interview pathway
why_included: Useful because it demonstrates the guaranteed-interview distinction and represents a nearby private pre-health/nursing-sensitive option.
scorecard_notes: Some earnings values came from secondary Scorecard-derived sources and should be verified directly. Major mix matters.
cost_notes: Sticker price is high, but school-reported aid may reduce net price. Actual family net price is essential.
prehealth_advising_notes: Seton Hall has a Pre-Professional Advising Center and pre-medical advising resources.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Strategically relevant because of Hackensack Meridian School of Medicine pathway, but the program guarantees an interview, not admission.
research_access_notes: Verify undergraduate research, nursing, health sciences, and clinical exposure opportunities.
early_assurance_notes: Joint Bachelor's/M.D. program with Hackensack Meridian guarantees an interview if requirements are met, not medical-school admission.
gpa_risk_notes: Premed requirements still apply; guaranteed interview does not remove the need for strong academic performance.
student_fit_notes: Nearby private university. Fit, net price, and intended major matter a lot.
parent_roi_note: Seton Hall is useful as a guaranteed-interview/private NJ comparison, but it should not be mistaken for a guaranteed direct-med seat.

name: University of Pittsburgh
strategic_role: Urban research university with major health-sciences ecosystem
why_included: Represents the out-of-state public health-sciences ecosystem lane and helps compare Rutgers/Rowan/Stony Brook against Pitt/UPMC.
scorecard_notes: Strong health-sciences ecosystem. Scorecard net price may not reflect Jeff's actual family cost as a NJ resident.
cost_notes: Out-of-state public cost likely needs a high bar compared with NJ public options.
prehealth_advising_notes: Pitt has a Pre-Health Resource Center supporting undergraduate pre-health students.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Major health-sciences ecosystem with UPMC and Pitt School of Medicine context.
research_access_notes: Strong research ecosystem; verify undergraduate access and fit.
early_assurance_notes: Pitt Medicine GAP is strategically important but still requires a high MCAT minimum.
gpa_risk_notes: Strong science/premed ecosystem may be competitive.
student_fit_notes: Urban Pittsburgh campus with major health-system adjacency.
parent_roi_note: Pitt is an ecosystem play, not a cheap OOS public. It needs to justify extra cost over NJ options.

name: Penn State University Park
strategic_role: Large regional flagship with broad option value
why_included: Represents a large flagship comparison against Rutgers, Maryland, Pitt, and Delaware.
scorecard_notes: Broad flagship outcome data should not be interpreted as premed-specific.
cost_notes: Out-of-state cost weakens the value case for a NJ family.
prehealth_advising_notes: Penn State has formal prehealth advising resources.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Less obviously an urban clinical ecosystem than Pitt, Temple, Drexel, or Stony Brook.
research_access_notes: Large research university with broad academic and extracurricular opportunity.
gpa_risk_notes: Large flagship environment; strong students need to self-navigate.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy wants a big college-town flagship experience.
parent_roi_note: Penn State is a broad brand/fallback option, but not clearly better premed ROI than Rutgers for a NJ family.

name: University of Maryland - College Park
strategic_role: Strong regional public research university with STEM and pre-health breadth
why_included: Represents a strong out-of-state public research option with excellent broad ROI signals.
scorecard_notes: Strong broad ROI signal, but Scorecard net price may not reflect NJ-family out-of-state cost.
cost_notes: Official nonresident COA is high despite attractive Scorecard net-price signal.
prehealth_advising_notes: UMD has a dedicated health professions advising office serving students and alumni across majors.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Strong regional/DC-adjacent opportunity, but not the same medical-center adjacency as Pitt, Temple, or Stony Brook.
research_access_notes: Strong research university with STEM and life-sciences breadth.
gpa_risk_notes: Strong public research environment; STEM/premed courses may be competitive.
student_fit_notes: Large research university near DC; campus and size fit matter.
parent_roi_note: UMD has excellent outcome signals, but the actual OOS price must beat the NJ public baseline.

name: University of Delaware
strategic_role: Regional public campus-fit and health-sciences comparison
why_included: Represents a familiar regional public option for NJ families, useful for campus-fit and health-sciences comparison.
scorecard_notes: Solid broad outcome signal, but OOS public sticker cost matters.
cost_notes: For a NJ family, Delaware may price closer to private-school territory unless merit or aid changes the math.
prehealth_advising_notes: UD has a Center for Health Profession Studies.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Health-sciences and advising ecosystem relevant; not as hospital-dense as urban medical-center schools.
research_access_notes: Verify health sciences, nursing, PA, and undergraduate research access.
gpa_risk_notes: Standard pre-health GPA risk; program-specific differential charges may matter.
student_fit_notes: Classic campus feel may appeal if Rutgers feels too large or urban options feel too intense.
parent_roi_note: Delaware needs a fit or aid reason to beat NJ publics.

name: Stony Brook University
strategic_role: SUNY research university with strong science and medical ecosystem
why_included: Represents a strong science/research SUNY option with Stony Brook Medicine ecosystem relevance.
scorecard_notes: Strong science/ROI signal; direct Scorecard API should verify exact graduation and earnings fields.
cost_notes: Still an out-of-state public for Lucy, but may be strategically relevant because of science and medical ecosystem.
prehealth_advising_notes: Stony Brook has formal pre-health advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Stony Brook Medicine ecosystem is a major reason for inclusion.
research_access_notes: Strong science/research university; useful for premed, biology, research, and health sciences.
gpa_risk_notes: Science-heavy environment may be competitive and GPA-sensitive.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes a science-heavy research university environment.
parent_roi_note: Stony Brook may offer a stronger medical/science ecosystem than some OOS publics, but it still needs to justify cost over NJ options.

name: Binghamton University
strategic_role: Strong SUNY academic/value comparison
why_included: Represents a strong regional public academic/value option, though less medically distinctive than Stony Brook or Pitt.
scorecard_notes: Strong broad academic/value signal; less clearly a medical-center ecosystem school.
cost_notes: Good public-value reputation, but not NJ in-state for Lucy.
prehealth_advising_notes: Binghamton has a dedicated Pre-Health Professions Advising Office.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Less obvious urban medical-center adjacency than Pitt, Temple, Drexel, Stony Brook, or Rutgers-Newark.
research_access_notes: Strong public academic environment; verify specific clinical and research access.
gpa_risk_notes: Strong academic environment; pre-health coursework still demanding.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes the campus and academic culture enough to justify OOS pricing.
parent_roi_note: Binghamton is a strong regional public comparison, but not obviously better than Rutgers or TCNJ for a NJ premed student.

name: Temple University
strategic_role: Urban Philadelphia clinical ecosystem and Temple Health pathway option
why_included: Represents urban clinical exposure, Temple Health, and an early-assurance-style pathway to LKSOM.
scorecard_notes: ROI signal is fair, not elite; Temple's inclusion is mainly for urban clinical/pathway comparison.
cost_notes: Temple cost varies by residency and college. Full COA should be verified before final scoring.
prehealth_advising_notes: Temple has Pre-Professional Health Advising for current students and alumni.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Urban Philadelphia clinical context and Lewis Katz School of Medicine pathway relevance.
research_access_notes: Verify undergraduate clinical/research access and how students use Temple Health connections.
early_assurance_notes: Temple Pre-Med Health Scholars begins after starting at Temple and still requires GPA and MCAT thresholds.
gpa_risk_notes: Urban pre-health environment with continuation requirements for special pathway.
student_fit_notes: Urban Philadelphia campus fit is central.
parent_roi_note: Temple is valuable as an urban clinical ecosystem comparison, not as a pure ROI winner.

name: Drexel University
strategic_role: Private urban co-op, pre-health, and health-tech fallback option
why_included: Represents the private urban co-op pathway and a BA/BS+MD early assurance program that is not fully guaranteed.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings signal likely reflects co-op, STEM, business, and career-oriented pathways. Not premed-specific.
cost_notes: High sticker price and complex quarter/co-op structure. Actual net price and intended path matter.
prehealth_advising_notes: Drexel has pre-health/preprofessional advising through Steinbright and related advising structures.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Urban Philadelphia healthcare/research context and Drexel College of Medicine pathway relevance.
research_access_notes: Co-op model may create distinctive experiential opportunities, but premed sequencing should be planned carefully.
early_assurance_notes: Drexel BA/BS+MD offers provisional early assurance but does not guarantee medical-school admission.
gpa_risk_notes: Co-op and quarter/calendar structure may complicate traditional premed sequencing.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes urban Philadelphia and experiential/co-op learning.
parent_roi_note: Drexel has strong career/earnings signals, but must be judged by net price and whether co-op genuinely helps Lucy's path.

name: Princeton University
strategic_role: NJ-adjacent ultra-elite general prestige school with strong need-based aid
why_included: Represents the elite general-prestige lane and is geographically relevant for a NJ family.
scorecard_notes: Very strong average-net-price and earnings signal, but average net price may not reflect Jeff's family-specific cost. Princeton has no medical school on campus.
cost_notes: Strong need-based aid, but likely no merit-aid strategy. Run net price calculator.
prehealth_advising_notes: Princeton has formal Health Professions Advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: No medical school on campus; value is elite undergraduate education, advising, research, and brand rather than hospital adjacency.
research_access_notes: Elite research university with strong undergraduate academic environment.
gpa_risk_notes: High academic expectations; premed GPA still matters.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy wants an intense, undergraduate-focused elite academic environment close to home.
parent_roi_note: Princeton is compelling if admitted and affordable, but it should not be treated as a medical-ecosystem school.

name: University of Pennsylvania
strategic_role: Ivy urban medical ecosystem with broad option value
why_included: Represents elite Ivy prestige plus a major urban academic medical ecosystem through Penn Medicine.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings may reflect major mix including business, finance, tech, and professional pathways. Not premed-specific.
cost_notes: Penn aid is need-based; no merit-aid strategy. Family-specific net price matters.
prehealth_advising_notes: Penn has pre-health advising and a Penn Letter process.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Penn Medicine and Philadelphia healthcare ecosystem are major differentiators.
research_access_notes: Elite research ecosystem with strong life-sciences, business, policy, and healthcare-adjacent options.
gpa_risk_notes: Intense Ivy/pre-professional environment; GPA and stress fit should be considered.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes urban Philadelphia, Ivy intensity, and strong pre-professional culture.
parent_roi_note: Penn is one of the clearest elite medical-ecosystem examples, but cost, admission odds, and fit are major constraints.

name: Johns Hopkins University
strategic_role: Elite biomedical research and premed-reputation school
why_included: Represents the elite biomedical/pre-med brand and high-intensity research ecosystem lane.
scorecard_notes: Some values came from secondary Scorecard-derived sources and should be verified directly. Hopkins' value is not fully captured by broad earnings metrics.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost. Need-aid policies may help some families; verify family-specific net price.
prehealth_advising_notes: Hopkins has an Office of Pre-Professional Programs and Advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: World-class biomedical and medical ecosystem; likely one of the strongest research/medicine environments in the set.
research_access_notes: Very strong biomedical research environment.
gpa_risk_notes: Potentially intense premed/science culture; fit and stress tolerance matter.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy genuinely wants a serious biomedical research and premed environment.
parent_roi_note: Hopkins is powerful but not necessary. It should be evaluated as an intense biomedical ecosystem, not a generic prestige upgrade.

name: Duke University
strategic_role: Elite private research university with Duke Health ecosystem
why_included: Represents elite private campus culture plus strong medical/research ecosystem outside the Northeast corridor.
scorecard_notes: Strong broad ROI and prestige signal. Earnings are broad school-level outcomes, not premed-specific.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost; need-based affordability should be checked.
prehealth_advising_notes: Duke has an Office of Health Professions Advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Duke Health ecosystem is a major strategic advantage.
research_access_notes: Strong elite research university with broad option value.
gpa_risk_notes: Elite academics and competitive prehealth environment; fit matters.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes Duke's campus culture and is comfortable farther from NJ.
parent_roi_note: Duke is a strong elite medical-ecosystem school, but for v1 it may be optional if we already include Penn/Hopkins/Stanford/Yale.

name: Brown University
strategic_role: Ivy open-curriculum school with PLME direct-med pathway
why_included: Represents the elite direct-med/open-curriculum lane through Brown PLME.
scorecard_notes: Brown regular undergrad and Brown PLME should be modeled as different strategies.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost; need aid matters.
prehealth_advising_notes: Brown has Health Careers Advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Brown has medical-school connections, but its main distinctive feature here is PLME and open curriculum.
research_access_notes: Elite undergraduate environment with flexible academic structure.
early_assurance_notes: Brown PLME is a direct-med strategy and must be separated from Brown regular premed.
gpa_risk_notes: Open curriculum may improve flexibility, but elite academic expectations remain.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy values academic flexibility and can make a credible medicine case early.
parent_roi_note: Brown earns v1 inclusion mainly because PLME teaches the direct-med strategy better than almost any other elite example.

name: Cornell University
strategic_role: Large Ivy research university with broad STEM/life-sciences option value
why_included: Represents a large Ivy research university with broad academic breadth but less urban medical adjacency.
scorecard_notes: Strong broad ROI and life-sciences signal; less direct urban clinical adjacency than some elite peers.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost; aid and college-specific costs should be checked.
prehealth_advising_notes: Cornell has a centralized Health Professions Advising Center.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Less immediate urban medical-center adjacency than Penn, Columbia, Yale, Duke, Stanford, or Hopkins.
research_access_notes: Large research university with strong life sciences, STEM, agriculture, and health-related academic options.
gpa_risk_notes: Large rigorous Ivy environment; GPA and fit should be considered.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes a large, rigorous, rural/college-town Ivy environment.
parent_roi_note: Cornell is strong, but may be less distinctive for this app than Penn, Hopkins, Brown, Stanford, MIT, or Princeton.

name: Columbia University
strategic_role: Ivy NYC medical/research ecosystem with Core Curriculum and urban intensity
why_included: Represents the Ivy NYC medical ecosystem and Core Curriculum/intensity tradeoff.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings and urban ecosystem signal; Core and NYC intensity should be treated as fit variables.
cost_notes: Very high sticker cost; need aid and family-specific net price matter.
prehealth_advising_notes: Columbia has preprofessional/prehealth advising and committee evaluation processes.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: NYC medical and research ecosystem is a major advantage.
research_access_notes: Elite urban research environment.
gpa_risk_notes: Core Curriculum plus urban intensity may add stress for some premed students.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy wants NYC, Core Curriculum, and an intense urban Ivy environment.
parent_roi_note: Columbia is a strong elite medical-ecosystem example, but fit risk may be higher than at more campus-contained schools.

name: Harvard University
strategic_role: Maximum broad prestige, research ecosystem, and option value
why_included: Represents the maximum general elite-prestige and broad option-value case.
scorecard_notes: Strong earnings and aid-adjusted value signal. Earnings reflect broad elite option value, not premed-specific outcomes.
cost_notes: Strong need-based aid, but not a merit-aid strategy. Net price calculator matters.
prehealth_advising_notes: Harvard has premed/prehealth advising for Harvard College students and alumni.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Boston/Cambridge biomedical and hospital ecosystem is very strong, though specific undergrad access should be verified.
research_access_notes: Extraordinary research and alumni ecosystem across medicine, science, policy, business, and tech.
gpa_risk_notes: Elite expectations and self-directed environment; GPA and fit still matter.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy would thrive in a highly elite, self-directed academic environment.
parent_roi_note: Harvard is maximum broad option value, but not necessary for med school.

name: Yale University
strategic_role: Ultra-elite liberal arts/research school with Yale-New Haven medical ecosystem
why_included: Represents elite liberal arts/research plus a strong medical ecosystem and aid profile.
scorecard_notes: Very strong elite outcomes and low-debt signal, but family-specific net price matters.
cost_notes: Need-based aid only; verify family net price.
prehealth_advising_notes: Yale has a Health Professions Advising Program.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Yale-New Haven medical ecosystem is a major differentiator.
research_access_notes: Elite liberal arts and research ecosystem.
gpa_risk_notes: Elite academic environment; premed fundamentals still matter.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy likes Yale's academic culture, New Haven, and residential-college environment.
parent_roi_note: Yale is a strong elite medical/research ecosystem example, but may be optional in v1 if Harvard/Penn/Hopkins/Stanford are already included.

name: Stanford University
strategic_role: Ultra-elite medicine, technology, biotech, AI, and innovation ecosystem
why_included: Represents the elite medicine-plus-technology/AI/biotech/innovation lane.
scorecard_notes: Very strong earnings and net-price signal. Graduation-rate discrepancies across sources should be verified directly.
cost_notes: Strong need-based aid for many families, but travel and distance from NJ matter.
prehealth_advising_notes: Stanford has pre-med/pre-health advising resources.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Stanford Medicine and Silicon Valley health-tech/biotech/AI context are major differentiators.
research_access_notes: Extraordinary medicine, tech, AI, biotech, startup, and research ecosystem.
gpa_risk_notes: Elite STEM/innovation environment may be intense but also high-option-value.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy is excited by medicine plus technology, biotech, AI, devices, or entrepreneurship and is comfortable far from home.
parent_roi_note: Stanford is the clearest health-tech/biomed innovation elite option, but it is far from NJ and ultra-selective.

name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
strategic_role: Ultra-elite STEM, engineering, computing, and health-tech pathway
why_included: Represents the highest-intensity STEM/engineering/health-tech version of a medicine-adjacent path.
scorecard_notes: Highest broad earnings signal, but this reflects STEM/tech outcomes and should not be read as premed-specific.
cost_notes: Strong aid for qualifying families, but not a merit-aid strategy.
prehealth_advising_notes: MIT has dedicated prehealth advising.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: Boston/Cambridge ecosystem is strong, but MIT should be framed primarily as STEM/health-tech rather than classic clinical premed.
research_access_notes: Extraordinary STEM, engineering, AI, computational biology, biomedical engineering, and health-tech research context.
gpa_risk_notes: High academic intensity and possible GPA pressure for premed.
student_fit_notes: Best only if Lucy genuinely loves STEM, math, computing, engineering, or health-tech intensity.
parent_roi_note: MIT has exceptional broad ROI and health-tech option value, but it may make classic premed harder than necessary if Lucy is not a STEM-intensity kid.

name: University of Chicago
strategic_role: Elite intellectual/research school with Core Curriculum and UChicago Medicine ecosystem
why_included: Represents the elite Core Curriculum/intellectual-rigor model with medical-center ecosystem.
scorecard_notes: Strong net-price and earnings signal, but official total COA and exact Scorecard fields should be verified.
cost_notes: Need-based aid policy and timing should be verified. Official total COA still needs cleanup.
prehealth_advising_notes: UChicago has Careers in Healthcare and health-professions advising structures.
clinical_ecosystem_notes: UChicago Medicine and Pritzker ecosystem are important, but undergrad access should be verified.
research_access_notes: Elite research university with intense intellectual culture.
gpa_risk_notes: Core Curriculum, quarter system, and academic intensity may create GPA/stress risk for premed.
student_fit_notes: Best if Lucy would enjoy intense academic culture and Core requirements.
parent_roi_note: UChicago is strategically useful as the elite rigor/Core example, but may not be essential for v1.
